source.title: Metabolic engineering of Methylobacterium extorquens AM1 for the production of butadiene precursor

initial_request.research_problem: Engineering Methylobacterium extorquens AM1 to produce crotyl diphosphate, a butadiene precursor, from crotonyl-CoA via a three-enzyme pathway (ADHE2, THK, IPK).
initial_request.known_context: No known enzymes for converting crotonyl-CoA to butadiene.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: No known enzymes for converting crotonyl-CoA to butadiene.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Lack of reports on enzymes catalyzing the biochemical reactions from crotonyl-CoA to butadiene.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: An in vitro one-pot strategy can identify promiscuous enzymes for sequentially converting crotonol into crotyl diphosphate.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Apply an in vitro one-pot strategy to identify promiscuous enzymes contributing to butadiene synthesis.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Strategy designed to efficiently identify promiscuous enzymes for butadiene synthesis.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Need to test candidate kinases for crotonol phosphorylation.

evidence.quote_or_span: One of the major challenges in this work is the lack of reports on enzymes catalyzing the biochemical reactions from crotonyl-CoA to butadiene.
Herein, an in vitro one-pot strategy was applied for sequentially converting crotonol into crotyl diphosphate.
this strategy is able to efficiently identify promiscuous enzymes contributing to butadiene synthesis

evolution_trajectory.state_before: No kinase identified for crotonol phosphorylation.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Which kinase can efficiently phosphorylate crotonol to crotyl monophosphate?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: THK is the most suitable kinase among MK, THK, TK for crotonol phosphorylation.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Test three kinase candidates (MK, THK, TK) for crotonol phosphorylation in M. extorquens AM1.
evolution_trajectory.observation: THK produced the highest crotyl monophosphate among candidates; background activity from native glycerate kinase detected (0.06 U/mg using 0.5 mM crotonol).
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Characterize THK kinetics to assess its efficiency.

evidence.quote_or_span: THK was the most suitable kinase among three candidates for phosphorylating crotonol to crotyl monophosphate in M. extorquens AM1.
crotyl monophosphate was also detected in the control, suggesting wild-type M. extorquens AM1 may contain one or more native enzymes capable of phosphorylating crotonol.
glycerate kinase in M. extorquens AM1 was able to phosphorylate crotonol with an activity of 0.06 U/mg using 0.5 mM crotonol as substrate

evolution_trajectory.state_before: THK selected as best kinase but activity unknown.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: What are the kinetic parameters of THK for crotonol?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: THK has low activity towards crotonol compared to its native substrate.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Determine Km and kcat of THK for crotonol.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Km=8.35 mM, kcat=1.24 s^-1; specific activity 0.1 U/mg, 300-fold lower than native substrate.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Low activity motivates protein engineering; also need second phosphorylation step.

evidence.quote_or_span: The values of $K_{m}$ and $k_{cat}$ were determined to be 8.35 mM and 1.24 s $^{-1}$
The specific activity of THK for 0.5 mM crotonol was 0.1 U/mg, which was 300-fold lower than that for the native substrate
The specific activity of THK for 0.5 mM crotonol was 0.1 U/mg, which was 300-fold lower than that for the native substrate

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Need enzyme for second phosphorylation step (crotyl monophosphate to crotyl diphosphate).
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Which enzyme can phosphorylate crotyl monophosphate?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: IPK from M. thermautotrophicus can phosphorylate crotyl monophosphate due to substrate similarity with its native substrate BEP.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Select IPK based on structural similarity of BEP and crotyl diphosphate.
evolution_trajectory.observation: IPK selected for crotyl monophosphate phosphorylation.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Combine with improved THK for in vitro one-pot reaction.

evidence.quote_or_span: The structures of BEP and crotyl diphosphate are quite similar

evolution_trajectory.state_before: THK has low activity (300-fold lower than native).
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How to improve THK activity towards crotonol?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Random mutagenesis and colorimetric screening can yield THK variants with higher activity.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform directed evolution of THK via random mutagenesis and high-throughput screening.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Variant THK M82V isolated with 8.6-fold higher activity than wild-type.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Characterize kinetic improvement and structural basis.

evidence.quote_or_span: the activity of one of rate-limiting enzymes, THK, was optimized by random mutagenesis coupled with a developed high-throughput screening colorimetric assay.
The resulting variant (THK $^{M82V}$ ) isolated from over 3000 colonies showed 8.6-fold higher activity than wild-type
The resulting variant (THK $^{M82V}$ ) isolated from over 3000 colonies showed 8.
The specific activity of THK for 0.5 mM crotonol was 0.1 U/mg, which was 300-fold lower than that for the native substrate

evolution_trajectory.state_before: THK M82V variant obtained but kinetic parameters unknown.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How does M82V mutation affect THK kinetics and structure?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: M82V relieves steric hindrance in substrate-binding pocket, improving kcat/Km.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Determine kinetic parameters of THK M82V and perform homology modeling.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Km value of THK M82V was determined to be 4.35 mM (from EV_AUTO_030), kcat/Km 12-fold higher than wild-type; M82 adjacent to loop 28–33, mutation relieves hindrance.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Use improved THK M82V in one-pot reaction with IPK.

evidence.quote_or_span: M82 is adjacent to the significant loop 28–33 (distance<5 Å) which is part of the substrate-binding pocket
When M82 was mutated to V82, the above-mentioned hindrance around M82 was relieved, likely improving the catalytic activity to the smaller molecule substrate such as crotonol
Directed evolution of the rate-limiting THK resulted in a variant (M82V) with the $k_{cat}/K_{m}$ value 12-fold higher than that of wild-type THK.
As a consequence, the $k_{cat}/K_{m}$ value of THK $^{M82V}$ was 12-fold higher than that of wild-type THK.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: THK M82V and IPK available but not tested together.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Can THK M82V and IPK convert crotonol to crotyl diphosphate in a one-pot reaction?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: One-pot reaction with THK M82V and IPK will produce crotyl diphosphate, but THK may still be rate-limiting.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform in vitro one-pot reaction with THK M82V and IPK, with enzyme replenishment.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Achieved 0.76 mM crotyl diphosphate (7.6% conversion); crotyl monophosphate barely detected, indicating THK still rate-limiting; 3.45-fold increase with enzyme replenishment.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Need to address crotonyl-CoA reduction step for full pathway.

evidence.quote_or_span: which helped increase the titer of crotyl diphosphate to 0.76 mM, corresponding to a 7.6% conversion from crotonol in the one-pot in vitro reaction.
the titer of crotyl diphosphate was increased to 0.76 mM at 6 h, corresponding to a 7.6% conversion
the titer of crotyl diphosphate was increased to 0.76 mM at 6 h, corresponding to a 7.6% conversion
crotyl monophosphate was barely detected in the whole time course (Fig. 6), suggesting THK was still a rate-limiting step
In our case, a similar phenomenon was observed by replenishing THK $^{M82V}$ and IPK at 2 h and 4 h, which increased the conversion rate by 3.45-fold.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Need enzyme for crotonyl-CoA reduction to crotonol.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Which enzyme can efficiently reduce crotonyl-CoA to crotonol?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: ADHE2 from C. acetobutylicum can reduce crotonyl-CoA more efficiently than FAR candidates.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Test ADHE2 and FAR enzymes for crotonyl-CoA reduction; determine kinetics.
evolution_trajectory.observation: ADHE2 selected; Km=2.34 mM, kcat=1.15 s^-1, kcat/Km=0.49 mM^-1 s^-1, lower than towards butanyl-CoA.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Combine all three enzymes in vivo in M. extorquens AM1.

evidence.quote_or_span: purified ADHE2 from C. acetobutylicum could reduce crotonyl-CoA to crotonol more efficiently compared with FAR from H. chejuensis and M. manganoxydans
This catalytic efficiency towards crotonyl-CoA $(k_{cat}/K_{\mathrm{m}}=0.49\ \mathrm{mM}^{-1}\ \mathrm{s}^{-1})$ was lower than that of ADHE2 towards butanyl-CoA

The $K_{m}$ and $k_{cat}$ of THK, IPK and ADHE2 were determined as 8.35 mM and 1.24 s $^{-1}$ , 1.28 mM and 153.14 s $^{-1}$ , and 2.34 mM and 1.15 s $^{-1}$ towards crotonol, crotyl monophosphate and crotonyl-CoA, respectively.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: All three enzymes (ADHE2, THK M82V, IPK) available but not tested in vivo.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Can overexpression of ADHE2, THK M82V, IPK in M. extorquens AM1 produce crotyl diphosphate from crotonyl-CoA or methanol?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Overexpression of the three enzymes will produce crotyl diphosphate from crotonyl-CoA or methanol.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Construct strain YCB3 overexpressing ADHE2, THK M82V, IPK under mxaF promoter; test production from crotonyl-CoA and methanol.
evolution_trajectory.observation: No crotyl diphosphate produced from crotonyl-CoA or methanol; no crotonol detected.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Test if pathway can function from exogenous crotonol; identify bottleneck.

evidence.quote_or_span: Overexpression of native ADHE2, IPK with THK $^{M82V}$ under a strong promoter mxaF in M. extorquens AM1 did not produce crotyl diphosphate from crotonyl-CoA
no crotyl diphosphate was produced by YCB3 during growth on methanol
No crotonol was detected either
Overexpression of native ADHE2, IPK with THK $^{M82V}$ under a strong promoter mxaF in M.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: In vivo pathway fails from crotonyl-CoA, but phosphorylation steps may work if crotonol is supplied.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Can the engineered strain produce crotyl diphosphate from exogenously supplied crotonol?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Supplying crotonol exogenously will bypass the reduction bottleneck and produce crotyl diphosphate.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Feed crotonol to YCB3 strain and measure intracellular crotyl diphosphate.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Produced 0.60 µg/mL (2.59 µM) intracellular crotyl diphosphate at mid-exponential phase.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Identify why crotonyl-CoA reduction fails in vivo.

evidence.quote_or_span: the engineered strain did generate 0.60 $\mu$ g/mL of intracellular crotyl diphosphate from exogenously supplied crotonol at mid-exponential phase.
About 0.60 $\mu$ g/mL (2.59 $\mu$ M) of intracellular crotyl diphosphate was accumulated at mid-exponential phase

evolution_trajectory.state_before: In vivo pathway fails from crotonyl-CoA but works from crotonol.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: What is the bottleneck preventing crotonyl-CoA reduction in vivo?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Low ADHE2 activity is the major bottleneck for crotonyl-CoA reduction.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform crude lysate assay of ADHE2 activity in YCB3.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Only 0.51 µg/mL crotonol (7.0 µM) produced at 4 h, confirming low ADHE2 activity.

evidence.quote_or_span: low activity of ADHE2 was one major bottleneck resulting in the lack of reduction of crotonyl-CoA into crotyl alcohol
0.51 $\mu$ g/mL of crotonol (7.0 $\mu$ M) was produced at 4 h

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Pathway partially functional but cannot produce from methanol.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Can the engineered strain produce crotyl diphosphate from methanol?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: The strain cannot produce from methanol due to low ADHE2 activity and insufficient EMC pathway flux.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Summarize limitations and propose future work.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Engineered M. extorquens AM1 cannot produce crotyl diphosphate from methanol; future work needed on ADHE2 and EMC pathway flux.

evidence.quote_or_span: The engineered M. extorquens AM1, however, cannot produce crotyl diphosphate from methanol yet. This was likely because of low activity of ADHE2 towards crotonyl-CoA reduction.

validation_methods: in vivo production assay with exogenous crotonol
metrics: crotyl diphosphate titer (0.76 mM in vitro, 0.60 µg/mL in vivo)
success_verification.final_conclusion: A three-enzyme pathway (ADHE2, THK M82V, IPK) was established for crotyl diphosphate production from crotonol, but production from methanol was not achieved due to low ADHE2 activity.
limitations: Low ADHE2 activity prevents crotonyl-CoA reduction in vivo
Cannot produce from methanol; requires further engineering of ADHE2 and EMC pathway flux
